Working tree is clean (git status shows nothing to commit)

wiki/ directory contains only .gitkeep - no wiki pages were ever generated

blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Implement the compileCommand function in cli/src/commands/kb.ts to: (1) Read all normalized .md files from company-hq/kb/normalized/, (2) Create company-hq/kb/wiki/index.md with conversation list (titles, dates, UUIDs), (3) Create individual topic pages in wiki/ directory, (4) Include source references in frontmatter (source conversation UUID, dates)

blockingIssues.description: Milestone assertions VAL-COMPILE-001 through VAL-COMPILE-004 CANNOT BE VALIDATED. These assertions require: index.md creation (VAL-COMPILE-001), topic pages creation (VAL-COMPILE-002), source references in frontmatter (VAL-COMPILE-003), and successful compile execution (VAL-COMPILE-004). None of these can pass when the command function doesn't exist.

conclusion: Round 5 scrutiny validation CONFIRMS: The kb-compile feature implementation is MISSING ENTIRELY. This is the FIFTH consecutive round with the SAME critical blocking issue. The compileCommand() function is referenced in the CLI command registration (registerKbCommands) but NEVER implemented. The kb.ts file shows: ingestCommand (defined), normalizeCommand (defined), outputCommand (defined twice), helpCommand (defined), but compileCommand is CALLED but NEVER DEFINED. Feature reviews were NOT spawned because there is nothing to review - the function does not exist. This requires URGENT orchestrator attention: either create a new fix feature with explicit implementation requirements, or assign to a worker who will actually implement compileCommand(). The feature has been incorrectly marked 'completed' for 5 validation rounds without any implementation code being written.
